items.headline: Energy storage demand grows 40% annually, lithium producers bet on growth drivers beyond EVs
items.snippet: Albemarle (ALB.US), the world's largest lithium producer, noted that demand for its energy storage business is steadily growing, contrasting with the irregularity of demand from electric vehicles. Grid energy storage is more evenly distributed globally, which is a very interestin

items.headline: Is Albemarle (ALB) One of the 10 Dividend Stocks with Low Payout Ratios and Strong Upside Potential?
items.snippet: On June 18, Citi upgraded Albemarle Corporation (NYSE:ALB) to Buy from Neutral with PT $225. Analyst Patrick Cunningham said the company is well-positioned to benefit from structural lithium demand tailwinds. Earlier RBC Capital raised price target to $257 with Outperform rating.

items.headline: Does Albemarle's Premium Valuation Justify Buying the Stock Now?
items.snippet: Zacks 2026 EPS consensus estimate $12.39, suggesting year-over-year rise of 1,668.4%. ALB expects lithium demand CAGR of 10-20% from 2025 to 2030. Energy Storage sales volumes up 14% YoY in Q1. Forward P/E 11.39 vs industry average 16.29. Zacks Rank #2 (Buy).

items.headline: Albemarle Likely to Continue Growing Volumes Over the Coming Years, RBC Says
items.snippet: Albemarle (ALB) is likely to continue growing volumes over the coming years from mostly brownfield at Wodgina, Atacama productivity, and opportunities at its Kings Mountain and Antofalla hubs, RBC Capital Markets said. Lithium supply is expected to remain tight through 2027. RBC

items.headline: Albemarle (ALB) Q1 2026 Earnings Transcript
items.snippet: First quarter net sales increased 70% year-over-year due to higher pricing and volumes. Adjusted EBITDA nearly tripled. Global Energy Storage Systems production rising 117% year over year. Company maintains full year 2026 outlook. On track to deliver $100M-$150M in cost and produ
